get.summary: Gets the users recent activities

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of items to retreive. Default 10, max 100.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The offset to start from. Default 0.

delete.summary:
Deletes a single unthreaded anchor for a document
If you need to delete a threaded anchor, see the delete comment handler

patch.summary: Edits a single anchor for a document

get.summary: Gets a set of comment anchors for a document

post.summary:
Creates an unthreaded anchor for a document
If you need to create a threaded anchor, see the create comment handler

delete.summary: Deletes a single comment for a document

patch.summary: Edits a single comment for a document

get.summary: Gets a set of comment threads for a document

post.summary:
Creates a single comment for a document
Optionally creates a new thread/anchor if one does not exist

post.description:
Batch-fetches lightweight previews for a list of call ids. Mirrors the
`POST /documents/preview` endpoint: no per-id access checks, duplicate
ids are deduplicated server-side, and missing ids come back as
`CallRecordPreview::DoesNotExist` rather than producing an error.

get.summary: Handler for `GET /call/record/{call_id}`.
get.description:
Returns the full [`CallRecord`] (metadata + participants + transcript)
for a call identified by its own id. Covers both active and archived calls.
Access is validated via channel membership (MemberParticipantRole).

delete.description:
Deletes a call record (and its participants/transcripts via cascade).
Access is validated via channel membership (MemberParticipantRole).

patch.description:
Edits a call record — currently supports updating the record's share
permissions. Access is validated via channel membership

post.description:
Toggles the `share_with_team` flag on the active call. Returns the new
value as the JSON body.

200.description: New value of share_with_team after toggle

patch.description:
Applies per-diarized-speaker `custom_speaker` overrides to the call's
archived transcript rows. Auth uses the same `EditAccessLevel` extractor
as `edit_call_record_handler`.

post.description:
Receives webhook events from the RTC provider (e.g. LiveKit).
The `Authorization` header contains the webhook auth token
and the body contains the raw event payload.

get.summary: Handler for `GET /call/{channel_id}`.
get.description:
Gets or creates a call for the channel. If a call already exists, joins it;
otherwise creates a new one. Always returns a join token.

get.summary: Handler for `GET /call/{channel_id}/active`.
get.description: Returns 200 with call info if an active call exists, or 204 No Content if not.

post.description:
Receives transcript segments from the transcription agent.
Authenticated via the `x-macro-internal-call` shared secret.
Duplicate segments (same `segment_id`) are ignored.

get.summary: Handler for `GET /channels/activity`.
Handler for `GET /channels/attachments/{entity_type}/{entity_id}/references`.
Handler for `GET /channels/{channel_id}`.
Handler for `GET /channels/{channel_id}/attachments`.

parameters.name: attachment_type
parameters.description: Filter by type: 'static' for images/videos, 'dss' for documents

get.summary: Handler for `GET /channels/{channel_id}/messages`.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Base64 encoded cursor value for older messages

parameters.name: previous_cursor
parameters.description: Base64 encoded cursor value for newer messages

parameters.name: load_around_message_id
parameters.description: Return a centered window around this message ID

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Base64 encoded cursor value for older messages

parameters.name: previous_cursor
parameters.description: Base64 encoded cursor value for newer messages

parameters.name: load_around_message_id
parameters.description: Return a centered window around this message ID

get.summary: Handler for `GET /channels/{channel_id}/messages/{message_id}/context`.

parameters.name: message_id
parameters.description: Message ID to get context around

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Number of older messages to include

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Number of newer messages to include

get.summary: Handler for `GET /channels/{channel_id}/messages/{message_id}/replies`.

parameters.name: message_id
parameters.description: Message ID (thread parent or reply id)

get.summary: Handler for `GET /channels/{channel_id}/messages/{message_id}/resolve`.
Handler for `GET /channels/{channel_id}/participants`.

delete.summary:
Soft-delete a CRM comment, scoped to the requesting user's team. When it
was the thread's last live comment, the thread is soft-deleted too
(reported via `threadDeleted`).

patch.summary:
Edit a CRM comment's text, scoped to the requesting user's team via the
comment's thread → entity → company. Returns the updated comment.

get.summary:
List the comment threads on a CRM company or contact, scoped to the
requesting user's team. Returns 404 when the entity isn't owned by the
team; an owned entity with no threads returns `200 []`.

parameters.name: entity_type
parameters.description: Which CRM entity kind the threads hang off

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: The CRM company or contact id

post.summary:
Create a comment on a CRM company or contact — a new thread, or a reply
when `threadId` is supplied. Returns the full thread (with all comments)
after the insert. Team-scoped; 404 when the entity isn't owned by the
team or `threadId` doesn't belong to it.

parameters.name: entity_type
parameters.description: Which CRM entity kind the thread hangs off

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: The CRM company or contact id

get.summary:
List the non-hidden contacts of a CRM company, scoped to the
requesting user's team. Returns 404 when the company isn't owned by
the team (so existence doesn't leak across teams); an owned company
with no visible contacts returns `200 []`.

parameters.name: company_id
parameters.description: The CRM company whose contacts to list

put.summary:
Toggle `email_sync` on a CRM company. `false` disables CRM email
sharing for the company and permanently removes its existing CRM
contacts and contact sources.

409.description: Company is hidden; un-hide before enabling email sync

put.summary:
Toggle `hidden` on a CRM company. Hiding also disables `email_sync`
and cascades to clearing the company's contacts and contact sources.

Toggle `hidden` on a CRM contact. Hiding is a display-only opt-out
scoped to the team that owns the contact's company.

get.summary: Gets the users documents to populate their recent document list

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of documents to retreive. Default 10, max 100.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The offset to start from. Default 0.

parameters.name: file_type
parameters.description: The file type to filter by. Default all.

post.description:
Creates a new document, generates an S3 presigned upload URL, and returns
the document metadata with the URL for the client to upload to.

contentType.description: Content type of the document converted from file type.

fileType.description: The file type of the document.

oneOf.description: Create document response data with content lifecycle metadata.

post.summary: Creates and initializes a markdown document in one backend-owned lifecycle.

schema.description: Response for creating a markdown document.

documentId.description: The document ID of the created markdown document.

post.summary:
Creates a task document with properties and initialized markdown content in
one backend-owned lifecycle.

documentId.description: The document ID of the created task.

teamId.description: The team this task number is scoped to.

teamTaskId.description: The task number assigned within the team.

get.summary: Gets a list of all the user's documents so they are able to be searched on

post.summary: Validates the provided document permissions token
Generates a document permissions token for a provided document id

put.summary:
Takes the docx document bom parts and generates presigned urls to upload
any new content

get.summary: Handler for `GET /documents/{document_id}`.
get.description: Returns document metadata, user access level, and view location.

put.summary: Creates a new version of a document

delete.description: Soft-deletes a document (only owners can delete).

patch.description:
Edits document metadata such as name or project, and modifies
the document's share permissions. Requires edit access to the document,
and edit access to the target project if moving the document.

get.summary: Handler for `GET /documents/{document_id}/branch_name`.
get.description:
Returns the short UUID and git branch name for a task document.
Returns 400 if the document is not a task.

post.description:
Copies an existing document, creating a new document with the same content.
Does not require re-uploading the document file.

parameters.name: version_id
parameters.description: The version id of the document to copy. Defaults to copying the latest version of the document.

get.summary:
Generates a presigned url to download the raw content of the document
For files with modification layers, they will not be applied in the downloaded file.
Handler for `GET /documents/{document_id}/github_prs`.
get.description:
Returns GitHub pull requests associated with a task document.
Returns 400 if the document is not a task.

get.summary: Gets the presigned url(s) for the document. aka location

parameters.name: document_version_id
parameters.description: A specific document version id to get the location for.

parameters.name: get_converted_docx_url
parameters.description: If true, this will return the converted docx url.

get.summary: Handler for `GET /documents/{document_id}/location_v3`.
get.description: Returns a presigned URL or sync service content for accessing the document.

parameters.name: document_version_id
parameters.description: A specific document version id to get the location for.

parameters.name: get_converted_docx_url
parameters.description: If true, this will return the converted docx url.

get.summary: Fetches the document pdf processing result for a given document id
Fetches the document processing result for a given document id and job id
Handler for `GET /documents/{document_id}/short_id`.
get.description: Returns the short UUID for a document.

put.summary:
For any file that isn't PDF or DOCX, use this endpoint for saving.
This endpoint will allow you to save the document by providing the file content as part of a multipart request. Use key 'file' along with the file bytes.
simple_save is different than the save_document endpoint because it does not return a presigned URL, Instead, it simply saves the file to S3 directly (and returns the document metadata).

get.summary: Gets the list of users who have viewed a given document
Gets a particular document by its id
Get the current user's permission for a given entity.

parameters.name: entity_type
parameters.description: Entity type (document, chat, project, thread, email_thread, channel)

get.summary: Get a visible foreign entity by its internal ID.
Redirects the user to the github sync app installation page.

307.description: Redirects to the github sync app installation page

get.summary: Health check
Gets the users history

post.summary: Upserts an item into the user's history and performs other necessary tracking actions

delete.summary: Deletes an item from the user's history

get.summary: Gets the instructions document for the current user

404.description: User does not have an instructions document

post.summary: Creates an instructions document for the current user

get.summary: Gets the items the user has access to

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: Whether to expand projects. Defaults to true.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Limit the number of items returned. Defaults to 20. Max 500.

parameters.name: sort_method
parameters.description: Sort method. Options are viewed_at, created_at, updated_at, viewed_updated. Defaults to viewed_at.

403.description: CRM-scoped queries require team membership, or requesting hidden CRM companies requires admin/owner team role

post.summary: Gets the items the user has access to

403.description: CRM-scoped queries require team membership, or requesting hidden CRM companies requires admin/owner team role

post.summary: Gets the items the user has access to using AST filters

403.description: CRM-scoped queries require team membership, or requesting hidden CRM companies requires admin/owner team role

get.summary: Gets the users pinned items

patch.summary: Saves the updated order of the pins for the user

post.summary: Pins the item for the user

delete.summary: Deletes the pin for the user

get.summary: Gets all the users projects. This includes projects shared with the user.

post.summary:
Creates a new project.
The project can be created as a sub-project of another project or as a top-level project.

get.summary: Gets all the users projects that are pending upload. This includes projects shared with the user.

post.summary:
Uploads a folder to the user's cloud storage. Mimicing the folder structure
with projects and placing all documents in the correct location.

destinationMap.description: maps document id to presigned url (external) or s3 info (internal)

fileSystem.description: file system with project ids and document ids

post.summary:
Creates a request id in the dynamodb table for tracking the upload
Returns a presigned url for uploading a zip file to the staging bucket
Returns a request id for tracking the upload

delete.summary:
Deletes a project.
Soft deletes the project and all of its children.

chat_ids.description: The ids of the chats that were marked as deleted

document_ids.description: The ids of the documents that were marked as deleted

project_ids.description: The ids of the project that were marked as deleted

get.summary: Gets the user's access level to the project

Gets the content of a project.
This includes the projects sub-projects as well as the items in the project.

delete.summary: Permanently deletes a project and all of it's children.

get.summary:
Gets the current documents share permissions
Gets the projects share permissions
Gets the users recently deleted items.

parameters.name: saved_view_id
parameters.description: The id of the saved view to delete

parameters.name: saved_view_id
parameters.description: The id of the saved view to patch

400.description: Malformed request or missing internal auth header

patch.summary: Edits the share permissions of a thread.

get.summary: Gets a UserPdfDocumentLocation entry

delete.summary: Deletes a document location for the user

get.summary: Gets the current documents share permissions

AccessLevel.description: Ordered from least to most access top -> bottom

ActivityType.description: The kind of activity a user performs in a channel.

ApiActivity.description: A user's activity (view/interaction) within a channel.

created_at.description: When the activity row was created.

interacted_at.description: The last time the user interacted with the channel.

updated_at.description: When the activity row was last updated.

viewed_at.description: The last time the user viewed the channel.

ApiAttachmentChannelReference.description: A reference to an attachment entity from a channel message.

attachment_created_at.description: When the attachment row was created.

channel_name.description: Optional channel name (DMs do not have a name).

message_content.description: Full message content (might be used for preview/snippet).

message_created_at.description: When the message itself was created.

message_id.description: Message that contains the attachment reference.

thread_id.description: If the message belongs to a thread this is the parent id.

oneOf.description: Referenced from a channel message.

allOf.description: Referenced from any non-message source entity.

oneOf.description: Referenced from any non-message source entity.

ApiAttachmentEntityReference.description: An attachment reference, tagged by source kind.

ApiAttachmentGenericReference.description: A reference to an attachment entity from a non-message source.

source_entity_type.description: Type of the source entity (e.g., "document", "chat", etc.).

user_id.description: User who created this reference (optional for non-user sources).

message_id.description: Message id this attachment belongs to.

sender_id.description: The user who sent the message containing this attachment.

next_cursor.description: Cursor for the next page, null if no more pages.

ApiChannelContextMessage.description: A channel message returned by the message-context endpoint.

updated_at.description: When the message was last updated.

ApiChannelDetail.description:
Channel detail: metadata, active participants, and a recent page of messages.

`messages` is the newest-first first page (size controlled by `limit`); use the
dedicated `/{channel_id}/messages` endpoint for cursor pagination.

channel_name.description: Resolved display name from the viewer's perspective.

ApiChannelMessage.description: A top-level channel message with thread info.

updated_at.description: When the message was last updated.

ApiChannelMessageKind.description: Position of a message in the channel/thread model.

next_cursor.description: Cursor for the next page, null if no more pages.

previous_cursor.description: Cursor for the previous page, null if no newer page exists.

ApiCountedReaction.description: A reaction with emoji and user list.

users.description: User ids who added this reaction.

callf.description: the filters that should be applied to the call entity

ccf.description:
Filters applied to the crm_company entity (wire key `ccf`).
Empty/omitted = team's full visible list.

cf.description: the filters that should be applied to the chat entity

chanf.description: the filters that should be applied to the channel entity

df.description: the filters that should be applied to the document entity

eca.description:
CRM-scoped address filter (wire key: `eca`). Symmetric counterpart
to `ecd` for fully-qualified email addresses.

ecd.description:
CRM-scoped domain filter (wire key: `ecd`). Parallel to the
freeform `ef` AST. Expanded by the router into an any-direction
OR sub-tree AND-merged into `ef`, plus a `CrmScope` tag stamped
on the resulting [`item_filters::ast::EmailFilterAst::crm_scope`].
Drives the per-team CRM authorization pre-check and candidate-set
widening downstream. Mutually exclusive with `eca`.

ef.description:
the filters that should be applied to the email entity (raw AST
tree only; CRM scope is carried by the `ecd` / `eca` sibling
fields). On this endpoint the email filter stays a bare tree,
unlike the materialized [`EntityFilterAst`] used for cursors.

fef.description: the filters that should be applied to foreign entity records

pf.description: the filters that should be applied to the project entity

propf.description: the filters that should be applied based on entity properties

ApiMessageSender.description: Public sender identity for channel messages.

id.description: Sender id without the storage namespace prefix.

ApiResolvedChannelMessage.description: Resolution metadata for any channel message id.

created_at.description: When the requested message was created.

kind.description: Whether the message is top-level or a thread reply.

thread_id.description: The top-level parent/thread id. Equals message_id for top-level messages.

preview.description: Last N replies for thread preview.

ApiThreadReply.description: A thread reply shown in preview.

updated_at.description: When the reply was last updated.

branchedFromId.description: The id of the document this document branched from

branchedFromVersionId.description:
The id of the version this document branched from
This could be either DocumentInstance or DocumentBom id depending on
the file type

createdAt.description: The time the document was created

deletedAt.description: The time the document was deleted

documentFamilyId.description: The id of the document family this document belongs to

documentVersionId.description:
The version of the document
This could be the document_instance_id or document_bom_id depending on
the file type

fileType.description: The file type of the document (e.g. pdf, docx)

projectId.description: The id of the project that this document belongs to

sha.description:
If the document is a PDF, this is the SHA of the pdf
If the document is a DOCX, this will not be present

oneOf.description:
The sub type of the document if present.
Task-related properties are encoded within the variant.

updatedAt.description: The time the document instance / document BOM was updated

oneOf.description: A task document with its associated properties

is_completed.description:
Whether the task is completed.
True if the Status property is set to "Completed".

BasicDocumentSubType.description:
Sub type of a document with associated properties encoded in each variant.
This ensures type-safety: task properties only exist when the document is a task.

id.description: The uuid of the bom part

path.description: The file path of the bom part content

sha.description:
The sha of the bom part content
There is an index on sha for more performant queries based on it.

branchName.description: The git branch name for the task document.

shortId.description: The short id of the document.

CallActiveResponse.description: Response indicating whether an active call exists for a channel.

channelId.description: The channel this call belongs to.

attended.description:
Filter by whether the requesting user attended the call.
`None` = no filter, `Some(true)` = only calls the user joined,
`Some(false)` = only calls the user did not join.

call_ids.description: Call record IDs to filter by. Empty to include all calls.

channel_ids.description: Channel IDs to filter calls by. Empty to include all calls.

speaker_ids.description: Speaker macro user ids. Empty to include all.

CallRecord.description:
Full record of a call, unifying rows from `calls` (active) and
`call_records` (archived) into a single response shape.

channelId.description: The channel this call belongs to.

channelName.description: Resolved display name for the channel.

customName.description:
User-supplied or AI-generated display name for the call. Only set on
archived `call_records`; active calls always return `None`.

durationMs.description: Call duration in milliseconds (None if still active).

endedAt.description: When the call ended (None if still active).

isActive.description: Whether the call is currently active (from `calls` table).

recordingStartedAt.description:
When the egress recording actually began. `None` until the
`egress_started` webhook arrives (typically a few seconds after
`started_at`). Frontend should anchor transcript-to-audio sync to
this value when present, falling back to `started_at` otherwise.

recordingUrl.description: Presigned URL for the call recording, if available.

shareWithTeam.description: Whether the call is shared with the creator's team.

startedAt.description: When the call started (created_at for active, started_at for archived).

summary.description:
AI-generated summary of the call. Only set on archived `call_records`
once summarization has run; active calls always return `None`.

CallRecordParticipant.description: A participant as returned in a [`CallRecord`] (historic — includes `left_at`).

joinedAt.description: When the user joined the call.

leftAt.description: When the user left (None if still in an active call).

allOf.description: The call exists (in either the active or archived table).

oneOf.description: The call exists (in either the active or archived table).

allOf.description: No call with this id exists in either the active or archived tables.

oneOf.description: No call with this id exists in either the active or archived tables.

CallRecordPreview.description:
Lightweight preview of a call record, returned by the batch preview endpoint.

Each requested id resolves to one of two outcomes: the call exists
(`Exists`) or it does not (`DoesNotExist`). The endpoint does not perform
access checks, so there is no separate "not authorized" variant.

CallRecordPreviewData.description: Preview payload returned for each found call id.

channelId.description: The channel this call belongs to.

channelName.description: Resolved display name for the channel.

customName.description:
User-supplied or AI-generated display name for the call. Only set on
archived `call_records`; active calls always return `None`.

endedAt.description: When the call ended (None if still active).

startedAt.description: When the call started (created_at for active, started_at for archived).

CallRecordTranscriptSegment.description: A transcript segment as returned in a [`CallRecord`].

diarizedSpeakerId.description:
Stable per-speaker identifier produced by the STT provider's diarization
pass. Unique across tracks in the call. `None` when the provider didn't
return a speaker label.

segmentId.description: LiveKit segment ID (nullable for archived records).

startedAt.description: When the speaker started this segment.

transcriptId.description: Stable DB-row id for the segment.

CallTokenResponse.description: Response returned when creating or joining a call.

channelId.description: The channel this call is associated with.

serverUrl.description: The RTC server URL for the frontend SDK to connect to.

token.description: The RTC token for connecting to the room.

ChannelFilters.description: The channel message filters used to filter down what channel messages you search over.

channel_ids.description: Channel IDs to search within. Examples: ['general']. Empty to search all accessible channels.

channel_types.description: Channel types to filter by. Examples: ['public'], ['direct_message', 'private']. Empty to search all channel types.

importance.description: Filter by channel importance. None to ignore, true to pass through (no clause), false to short-circuit and return nothing.

mentions.description: Channel user mentions to search for. Examples: ['@username']. Empty if not filtering by mentions.

org_id.description: Channel organization ID to search within. Empty to ignore organization filtering.

sender_ids.description: Sender IDs to search within. Examples: ['user1']. Empty to search all accessible senders.

team_id.description: Channel team ID to search within. Empty to ignore team filtering.

thread_ids.description: Channel thread IDs to search within. Examples: ['thread123']. Empty to search all threads.

activity_after.description:
When set, only return top-level messages with channel activity at or after
this timestamp. Activity means either the message itself was created after
this time, or a thread reply was created after this time.

Accepts the legacy JSON field `last_activity` for backwards compatibility.

activity_before.description:
When set, only return top-level messages with channel activity before this
timestamp. Activity means either the parent message or at least one thread
reply falls in the requested activity window.

created_after.description: When set, only return top-level messages created at or after this timestamp.

created_before.description: When set, only return top-level messages created before this timestamp.

message_ids.description: When non-empty, only return messages with these IDs.

notification_filters.description:
When set, only return top-level messages where the message itself or
any active thread reply has a notification for the requesting user that
matches these notification state constraints.

oneOf.description: Viewer can access the channel.
Viewer cannot access the channel.
Channel does not exist.

ChannelPreview.description: Preview entry for a single channel id.

ChannelPreviewData.description: Preview payload returned for accessible channels.

access_level.description: The access level for the channel

createdAt.description: The time the chat was created

deletedAt.description: The time the chat was deleted

model.description: The model used to generate the chat

projectId.description: The project id of the chat

updatedAt.description: The time the chat was last updated

ChatFilters.description: The chat filters used to filter down what chats you search over.

chat_ids.description: Chat ids to search over. Examples: ['chat1'], ['chat1', 'chat2']. When provided, chat search will only match results on these chats. Empty to search all accessible chats.

importance.description: Filter by chat importance. None to ignore, true to pass through (no clause), false to short-circuit and return nothing.

owners.description: Filter by chat owner. Examples: ['macro|user1@user.com'], ['macro|user1@user.com', 'macro|user2@user.com']. Empty to search all owners.

project_ids.description: A list of project ids to search within. Examples: ['project1']. Empty to ignore project filtering.

role.description: Chat message roles to search. Examples: ['user'], ['assistant']. Empty to search all roles.

CopyDocumentQueryParams.description: Query parameters for the copy document endpoint.

version_id.description: The DB version id of the document to copy. Defaults to latest.

CopyDocumentRequest.description: Request body for copying a document.

documentName.description: The name of the new document (without extension).

oneOf.description: Optional sync service version ID for MD documents.

CopyDocumentResponse.description: Response wrapper for the copy document endpoint.

oneOf.description:
The created documents metadata
Only returned if the document was created successfully

The presigned url used to upload the document
Only returned if the document was created successfully

success.description: Indicates if the document was created successfully

participants.description: Participants to add, excluding the owner.

CreateChannelResponse.description: Response returned after creating a channel.

metadata.description: Arbitrary client metadata for the comment.

threadId.description:
Existing thread to append to. Omit to start a new thread on the
addressed entity.

threadMetadata.description:
Metadata to set on a newly created thread (ignored when replying
without a value).

branchedFromId.description: The document id if the document is being branched.

branchedFromVersionId.description: The version id if the document is being branched.

createdAt.description: Optional time to set the document's created_at to. Set when backfilling email attachments.

documentFamilyId.description: The document family id if the document is being branched.

documentName.description: The name of the document without extension.

emailAttachmentId.description:
Internal only field that links the document created to the specified email attachment by
creating a row in the document_email table.

fileType.description: Optional file type of the document.

id.description: The id of the document in the database

isTask.description:
Whether the document is a task.
This is only applicable for md documents and the request will fail if the file type is not
md.

jobId.description:
Optional job id to be used to track an upload job for the newly created document.
Will need to have a corresponding job initiated for the file beforehand.

mimeType.description: The content type of the document (currently only used for logging matches against file type).

skipHistory.description: Whether to add a viewed_at record for this document upon creation.

teamId.description:
Team to assign the task number within when creating a task document. If omitted,
the service may infer it only when the creator belongs to exactly one team.

contentType.description: Content type of the document converted from file type

fileType.description: The file type of the document

source_entity_id.description: Id of the entity that owns the mention.

source_entity_type.description: Type of the entity that owns the mention.

source_entity_id.description: Id of the entity that owns the mention.

source_entity_type.description: Type of the entity that owns the mention.

CreateMarkdownDocumentRequest.description:
Request body for creating a markdown document whose content is initialized
by the backend.

markdown.description: Markdown source text. Defaults to an empty document.

projectId.description: Optional project ID to associate the document with.

skipHistory.description: Whether to add a viewed_at record for this document upon creation.

CreateMarkdownDocumentResponse.description: Response for creating a markdown document.

documentId.description: The document ID of the created markdown document.

projectParentId.description: The project that the new project will belong to.

CreateTaskRequest.description: Request body for creating a task.

markdown.description: Markdown source text. Defaults to an empty task document.

projectId.description: Optional project ID to associate the task with.

propertyValues.description: Optional property values to set on the task.

shareWithTeam.description:
Whether to share the task with your team or not
Defaults to true

teamId.description:
Team to assign the task number within. If omitted, it is inferred only
when the creator belongs to exactly one team.

documentId.description: The document ID of the created task.

teamId.description: The team this task number is scoped to.

teamTaskId.description: The task number assigned within the team.

deletedAt.description: When the comment was soft-deleted, if ever.

order.description:
Optional explicit ordering within the thread; the frontend falls
back to `createdAt` when absent.

owner.description: Macro user id of the comment author.

sender.description: Macro user id of the actual sender, when distinct from `owner`.

threadId.description: The id of the thread this comment belongs to.

updatedAt.description: When the comment was last updated.

CrmCommentEntityType.description:
Which CRM entity a comment thread is attached to. Serializes to
`crm_company` / `crm_contact` — matching the `entityType` the frontend
uses elsewhere when building entity URLs — and is parsed from the
`{entity_type}` path segment on the comment routes.

CrmCommentThread.description:
A [`CrmThread`] with its comments nested under it — the unit the
frontend renders.

CrmCompanyFilters.description: The crm company filters used to narrow which CRM companies appear in soup.

company_ids.description:
CRM company ids to filter by. Examples: ['11111111-...']. Empty to
include all of the team's visible CRM companies.

hidden.description:
Optional `crm_companies.hidden` filter. `None` = visible only
(default for back-compat with non-admin callers). `Some(false)` =
visible only (explicit). `Some(true)` = hidden only — requires
admin/owner team role; enforced upstream in soup's axum router.

CrmContactResponse.description: A CRM contact as returned by `GET /crm/companies/{company_id}/contacts`.

companyId.description: The id of the company the contact belongs to.

createdAt.description: When the contact record was created.

firstInteraction.description: Earliest known interaction with this contact.

id.description: The id of the contact record.

lastInteraction.description: Most recent known interaction with this contact.

name.description: Display name observed for the contact, if any.

updatedAt.description: When the contact record was last updated.

CrmThread.description: A CRM comment thread: the parent record one or more comments hang off.

deletedAt.description: When the thread was soft-deleted, if ever.

entityId.description: The id of the CRM company or contact this thread belongs to.

entityType.description: Which CRM entity kind this thread belongs to.

owner.description: Macro user id of the thread creator.

updatedAt.description: When the thread was last updated.

CustomSpeakerAssignment.description:
One per-diarized-speaker override, used in [`EditCallTranscriptRequest`].

`custom_speaker = None` clears any existing override for this
`diarized_speaker_id`; `Some(macro_user_id)` sets it. The string is
expected to parse as a `MacroUserId` (e.g. `macro|alice@example.com`);
the service layer rejects malformed values with `400 Bad Request`.

customSpeaker.description: Macro user id to attribute matching rows to, or `None` to clear.

diarizedSpeakerId.description: The diarization label whose rows this assignment targets.

DataType.description: Data type for property values, determining storage and validation.

DeleteCrmCommentResult.description:
Outcome of soft-deleting a CRM comment: reports whether the parent thread
was soft-deleted too (it is when the deleted comment was its last live one).

threadDeleted.description:
Whether the thread itself was soft-deleted because no live comments
remained.

threadId.description: The thread the comment belonged to.

DeleteMessageQuery.description: Query parameters for deleting a message.

DocumentBasic.description: Returns basic information of a document used for some db queries

DocumentContent.description: API-visible content lifecycle and location metadata.

oneOf.description: The content location, when known.

state.description: The current lifecycle state of the content.

DocumentContentLocation.description: Where document content is, or is expected to be, read from.

DocumentContentState.description: API-visible content lifecycle state derived from current document metadata.

DocumentFilters.description: The document filters used to filter down what documents you search over.

document_ids.description: Document ids to search over. Examples: ['doc1'], ['doc1', 'doc2']. Empty to search all accessible documents.

file_types.description: Document file types to search. Examples: ['pdf'], ['md', 'txt']. Empty to search all file types.

importance.description: Filter by document importance. None to ignore, true to pass through (no clause), false to short-circuit and return nothing.

is_email_attachment.description: Filter by email attachment status. true = only email attachments, false = only non-email attachments, None = both.

owners.description: Filter by document owner. Examples: ['macro|user1@user.com'], ['macro|user1@user.com', 'macro|user2@user.com']. Empty to search all owners.

project_ids.description:
A list of project ids to search within. Examples: ['project1'].
filtering. Empty to ignore project filtering.

sub_types.description: Filter by document sub type. Examples: ['task']. Empty to search all sub types.

task_filters.description: Task-specific filters that only apply to task subtype documents.

branchedFromId.description: The id of the document this document branched from

branchedFromVersionId.description:
The id of the version this document branched from
This could be either DocumentInstance or DocumentBom id depending on
the file type

createdAt.description: The time the document was created

deletedAt.description: The time the document was deleted

documentBom.description: If the document is a DOCX document and unzipped, the document_bom will be present

documentFamilyId.description: The id of the document family this document belongs to

documentVersionId.description:
The version of the document
This could be the document_instance_id or document_bom_id depending on
the file type

fileType.description: The file type of the document (file extension)

modificationData.description:
The modification data for the document instance.
This is only used for PDF documents.

projectId.description: The id of the project that this document belongs to

projectName.description: The name of the project that this document belongs to

sha.description:
If the document is a PDF, this is the SHA of the pdf
If the document is a DOCX, this will not be present

oneOf.description: The sub type of the document if present.

updatedAt.description: The time the document instance / document BOM was updated

teamId.description: The team this task number is scoped to, for task documents.

teamTaskId.description: The task number assigned within the team, for task documents.

DocumentMetadataWithContent.description: Full document metadata plus content lifecycle metadata.

access_level.description: The access level of the user for the document

exp.description: The expiration time of the token

file_type.description: The file type of the document (e.g. pdf, docx)

owner.description: The id of the owner of the document

oneOf.description:
The sub type of the document if present.
Task-related properties are encoded within the variant.

updated_at.description: The time the document was last updated

oneOf.description: A task document with completion status

is_completed.description:
Whether the task is completed.
True if the Status property is set to "Completed".

DocumentPreviewDataSubType.description:
The sub type of a document preview with associated properties.
Task-related properties are encoded within the variant to ensure valid states.

DocumentResponse.description: Document response with content lifecycle metadata.

presignedUrl.description: Presigned upload URL, when the caller still needs to upload bytes.

branchedFromVersionId.description:
The id of the version this document branched from
This could be either DocumentInstance or DocumentBom id depending on
the file type

createdAt.description: The time the document was created

documentBom.description: If the document is a DOCX document, the document_bom will be present

documentFamilyId.description: The id of the document family this document belongs to

documentVersionId.description:
The version of the document
This could be the document_instance_id or document_bom_id depending on
the file type

fileType.description: The file type of the document

modificationData.description:
The modification data for the document instance.
This is only used for PDF documents.

sha.description:
If the document is a PDF, this is the SHA of the pdf
If the document is a DOCX, this will not be present

oneOf.description: The sub type of the document if present.

updatedAt.description: The time the document instance / document BOM was updated

teamId.description: The team this task number is scoped to, for task documents.

teamTaskId.description: The task number assigned within the team, for task documents.

DocumentResponseMetadataWithContent.description: Create/copy response metadata plus content lifecycle metadata.

DocumentSubType.description:
The document sub type enum represents all values of document sub types.
These values should match the `document_sub_type_value` table in macrodb.

customName.description:
Updated user-supplied display name for the call. `None` is a no-op;
`Some("")` clears `call_records.custom_name`; any other `Some(s)`
overwrites it with `s`. Only the archived `call_records` row carries
this column — patching while the call is still active is a no-op for
this field.

oneOf.description: Updated share permissions.

shareWithTeam.description:
If `Some(true)`, grant the creator's team View access on the call.
If `Some(false)`, revoke the creator's team's access. `None` is a no-op.
The team is resolved from the call's `created_by`, not the acting user.

EditCallTranscriptRequest.description:
Body of `PATCH /call/record/{call_id}/transcript`.

Each entry in `assignments` sets (or clears, when `custom_speaker` is
`None`) the `custom_speaker` override for every transcript row in the
call whose `diarized_speaker_id` matches. Diarized speakers not listed
are left untouched. Empty `assignments` is a 204 no-op.

assignments.description: The set of per-diarized-speaker overrides to apply.

EditDocumentServiceArgs.description: Arguments for the edit_document service call.

oneOf.description: The new file type for the document (null to clear).

projectId.description: The new project id of the document.

oneOf.description: Updated share permissions for the document.

EmailFilters.description: The email filters used to filter down what emails you search over.

bcc.description: Email BCC addresses to filter by. Examples: ['user@example.com']. Empty if not filtering by BCC.

calendar_only.description:
When `Some(true)`, only include threads that have at least one message
with an iCalendar attachment (`.ics` filename or `application/ics` mime
type). `Some(false)` and `None` apply no constraint.

cc.description: Email CC addresses to filter by. Examples: ['user@example.com']. Empty if not filtering by CC.

crm_addresses.description:
CRM-scoped address filter. When non-empty, expands visibility to every
teammate's mailbox and restricts to threads involving any of these
fully-qualified addresses. Each address is authorized against
`crm_contacts` + `crm_companies` (contact must not be hidden, company
must not be hidden, `email_sync` must be true).
Mutually exclusive with `crm_domains`.

crm_domains.description:
CRM-scoped domain filter. When non-empty, expands visibility to every
teammate's mailbox and restricts to threads involving any of these
domains (in any of sender/cc/bcc/recipient). Each domain is authorized
against `crm_domains` + `crm_companies` (must exist for the caller's
team, company must not be hidden, `email_sync` must be true).
Mutually exclusive with `crm_addresses`.

email_thread_ids.description: Email thread IDs to filter by. Examples: ['thread-uuid-1']. Empty to search all threads.

exclude_labels.description:
Exclude emails that have any of these labels. Supports both Gmail system labels (e.g. "CATEGORY_PROMOTIONS") and user-created labels. Empty to not exclude any labels.
Note: SPAM and TRASH emails are not indexed in OpenSearch, so they are already excluded by default.

importance.description:
Filter by email importance. None to not filter. True to show only important emails
(drafts, personal, sent, or uncategorized). False to show only unimportant emails
(those categorized as promotions, social, updates, or forums).

include_labels.description:
Only include emails that have at least one of these labels. Supports both Gmail system labels (e.g. "INBOX", "CATEGORY_PROMOTIONS") and user-created labels (e.g. "github"). Empty to not filter by included labels.
Note: SPAM and TRASH emails are not indexed in OpenSearch, so they will never appear in results regardless of this filter.

link_ids.description:
Restrict to specific inboxes by email_links.id. Empty means "any inbox the
caller can access" (soup expands to the full set at the router edge).

project_ids.description: A list of project ids to search within. Empty to ignore project filtering.

recipients.description: Email Recipient addresses to filter by. Examples: ['user@example.com']. Empty if not filtering by Recipient.

senders.description: Email sender addresses to filter by. Examples: ['user@example.com']. Empty to search all senders.

shared.description:
Controls whether shared email threads are included in results.
Defaults to "exclude" (only the user's own threads).

EmptyResponse.description: Empty response is required due to custom fetch forcing `response.json()`

EntityFilters.description: a bundle of all of the filters for each entity type

property_filters.description: property-based filters applied across entity types

oneOf.description: Permission for item-based entities (document, chat, project, thread).

access_level.description: The access level the user has.

oneOf.description: Permission for channel-based entities.

role.description: The role the user has in the channel.

oneOf.description: Permission for team-based entities.

role.description: The role the user has in the team.

EntityPermission.description:
A user's permission for an entity, discriminated by entity kind.

Items (documents, chats, projects, threads) use access levels.
Channels use participant roles.

EntityPermissionResponse.description: API response envelope for entity permissions.

EntityReference.description: Entity reference for entity-type property values.

specific_message_id.description:
For CHANNEL, CHAT, THREAD entity types - optional specific message ID.
This allows referencing a specific message within a thread/channel/chat.

EntityType.description: Type of entity that can be referenced by entity properties.

ErrorResponse.description:
A plain old json error response for use with axum.
yup, thats it.

ExcludedDefaultView.description:
Frontend can define any set of its own default views for the user.
This is a list of views that are excluded from the default views list on the frontend.

It is important that the frontend can quickly iterate on default views, for that reason
we don't keep track of default views in the database, only those that are explicitly excluded.

presigned_url.description: The presigned url to download the raw content of the document

FileType.description:
Generates a FileType enum and associated ContentType enum with their implementations.

This macro takes a list of tuples in the format:
(Variant, "extension", "mime_type", CONTENT_TYPE_VARIANT)

For each tuple it generates:
- A variant in the FileType enum
- A variant in the ContentType enum
- Implementations for:
  - FileType::to_str() - Converts FileType to extension string
  - FileType::from_str() - Converts extension string to FileType
  - From<FileType> for ContentType - Maps FileType to ContentType
  - ContentType::mime_type() - Gets MIME type for ContentType

oneOf.description: Set the file type to a specific value.

set.description: Set the file type to a specific value.

oneOf.description: Clear the file type (set to null).

FileTypeUpdate.description: Represents a file type update: either set to a specific type or clear to null.

oneOf.description: The file type of the file

fullName.description: The full OS name of the file for deduplication

name.description: The name of the file, without the extension

relativePath.description:
The relative path of the file.

This is the `webkitRelativePath` with the name of the file stripped at the end.

ForeignEntity.description: A persisted mapping to an entity owned by an external system.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the record was created.

foreignEntityId.description: Identifier assigned by the external system.

foreignEntitySource.description: Source system that owns the external identifier.

id.description: Internal primary key for this foreign entity record.

metadata.description: Arbitrary metadata stored with the mapping.

storedForAuthEntity.description: Internal auth entity namespace this foreign entity is stored for.

storedForId.description: Internal entity identifier this foreign entity is stored for.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the record was last updated.

foreign_entity_ids.description: External entity identifiers to filter by. Empty to include all external IDs.

foreign_entity_sources.description: External source names to filter by. Empty to include all sources.

ids.description: Internal foreign entity record IDs to filter by. Empty to include all records.

success.description: Indicates if the request was successful

oneOf.description: Data to be returned

references.description: References to the requested entity, newest-first.

GetBatchCallRecordPreviewRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /call/record/preview`.

The `call_ids` list is bounded at [`MAX_BATCH_CALL_IDS`]; the handler
rejects anything larger with a 400 before touching the database.

callIds.description:
The call ids to preview. Duplicate ids are deduplicated server-side.
Capped at [`MAX_BATCH_CALL_IDS`] entries.

previews.description: One entry per deduplicated input id.

GetBatchChannelPreviewResponse.description: Response for a batched channel preview lookup.

previews.description: Resolved channel previews, one per requested channel id.

branchedFromId.description: The id of the document this document branched from

The id of the version this document branched from
This could be either DocumentInstance or DocumentBom id depending on
the file type

documentFamilyId.description: The id of the document family this document belongs to

documentVersionId.description: The id of the document version

fileType.description: The file type of the document

updatedAt.description: The time the document instance / document BOM was updated

userAccessLevel.description: The users level of access to the document

viewLocation.description: The users view location if there is one

userAccessLevel.description: The user's access level to the document

messages.description: Messages around the requested message in chronological order.

GetOrCreateAction.description: Result of a get-or-create channel operation.

action.description: Whether the channel was fetched or created.

GetOrCreateDmRequest.description: Request to get or create a direct message channel.

GetOrCreatePrivateRequest.description: Request to get or create a private channel.

data.description: DSS Items inside of a given project with their user access level attached to each item
The project metadata and user access level

userAccessLevel.description: The users level of access to the project

GithubPullRequest.description: Display-ready data for a GitHub pull request associated with a task.

additions.description: The number of added lines in the pull request, when enrichment data is available.

checks.description: Check runs collected from the pull request head commit, when enrichment data is available.

comments.description: Comments collected from the pull request, when enrichment data is available.

deletions.description: The number of deleted lines in the pull request, when enrichment data is available.

displayName.description: A compact label suitable for display in the UI.

githubKey.description: The stored GitHub association key, in `owner/repo/pull/number` format.

name.description: The GitHub pull request title, when enrichment data is available.

owner.description: The GitHub repository owner or organization.

status.description: The GitHub pull request status, when enrichment data is available.

url.description: The public GitHub URL for the pull request.

GithubPullRequestCheckRun.description: A check run associated with a GitHub pull request.

completedAt.description: When the check run completed, when available.

conclusion.description: The raw GitHub check run conclusion, when the run has completed.

id.description: The unique GitHub identifier for the check run.

startedAt.description: When the check run started, when available.

status.description: The raw GitHub check run status.

url.description: The public GitHub URL for the check run, when available.

GithubPullRequestComment.description: A comment associated with a GitHub pull request.

authorAssociation.description: GitHub's relationship label for the author, when available.

authorLogin.description: The GitHub login for the comment author, when available.

body.description: The comment or review body text.

createdAt.description: When the comment was created or the review was submitted.

id.description: The unique GitHub identifier for the comment or review.

source.description: The GitHub source for the comment, such as `issue_comment` or `review_comment`.

updatedAt.description: When the comment or review was last updated.

url.description: The public GitHub URL for the comment or review, when available.

GithubPullRequestsResponse.description: Response containing all GitHub pull requests associated with a task.

pullRequests.description: Parsed pull requests, in repository query order.

LeaveCallResponse.description: Response for the leave/end call operation.

callEnded.description: Whether the entire call was ended (room deleted).

oneOf.description: The presigned url of the document if it is not a docx

presignedUrl.description: The presigned url of the document if it is not a docx

oneOf.description: The presigned urls of the docx bom parts if it is a docx

Multiple document-storage URLs, currently for DOCX BOM parts.
Sync-service backed content.

LocationResponseV3.description: Location response with content lifecycle metadata.

NewChannelAttachment.description: New attachment to add to a channel message.

NotificationFilters.description: Notification state filters for channel message queries.

done.description:
Filter by notification done state. `Some(true)` selects done
notifications; `Some(false)` selects not-done notifications.

seen.description:
Filter by notification seen state. `Some(true)` selects seen
notifications; `Some(false)` selects not-seen notifications.

expand.description: Whether to expand projects. Defaults to true.

limit.description: Limit the number of items returned. Defaults to 20. Max 500.

oneOf.description: Sort method. Options are viewed_at, created_at, updated_at, viewed_updated. Defaults to viewed_at.

ParticipantRole.description: The role a user has within a channel.

PatchMessageRequest.description: Request to patch a channel message.

name.description: The new name of the project.

projectParentId.description: The new project that the new project will belong to.

oneOf.description: The share permissions for the project.

activity_type.description: The kind of activity to record.

channel_id.description: Channel id to record activity for.

The updated BOM for the document
Containing the file path and the sha

createdAt.description: The time the project was created

updatedAt.description: The time the project was updated

userId.description: The user id of who created the project

chat_ids.description: The ids of the chats that were marked as deleted

ProjectFilters.description: The project filters used to filter down what projects you search over.

importance.description: Filter by project importance. None to ignore, true to pass through (no clause), false to short-circuit and return nothing.

include_root.description: When true, `project_ids` also matches the projects themselves in addition to their children.

owners.description: Filter by project owner. Examples: ['macro|user1@user.com'], ['macro|user1@user.com', 'macro|user2@user.com']. Empty to search all owners.

project_ids.description:
Project IDs to search within. Examples: ['project1']. Empty to search all accessible projects.
By default matches children of these projects; set `include_root` to also match the projects themselves.

is_metadata.description:
Flag to indicate if this is a system-generated metadata property.
Not stored in database - computed at service layer.

is_system.description: Flag to indicate if this is a system property (stored in DB).

PropertyFilter.description:
A single property-based filter condition.

Each filter targets a specific property definition on entities of a given type,
matching against select option UUIDs or entity reference IDs.
Multiple values within a single filter are OR'd together.
Multiple filters are AND'd together.

entity_ids.description: Entity reference IDs to match. Multiple values are OR'd together.

entity_type.description:
The entity type for the property lookup (e.g., "TASK", "DOCUMENT", "PROJECT").
When None, matches across all entity types.

option_ids.description: Select option UUIDs to match. Multiple values are OR'd together.

property_definition_id.description: The UUID of the property definition to filter on.

PropertyInput.description: Property input for setting a property value on a task.

value.description: The value to set for the property.

oneOf.description: User-scoped property only
Organization-scoped property only
Both user and organization-scoped
System-owned property (no user or org owner)

PropertyOwner.description: Defines who owns a property - user-scoped, org-scoped, system, or both user and org.

oneOf.description:
Boolean value
Serializes as: {"type": "Boolean", "value": true}

Numeric value
Serializes as: {"type": "Number", "value": 42.5}

String value
Serializes as: {"type": "String", "value": "text"}

Date/timestamp value
Serializes as: {"type": "Date", "value": "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z"}

Select option(s) - always an array (check is_multi_select to determine if single or multi)
Single-select: {"type": "SelectOption", "value": ["uuid"]} (length 0 or 1)
Multi-select: {"type": "SelectOption", "value": ["uuid1", "uuid2", ...]} (length 0+)

value.description:
Select option(s) - always an array (check is_multi_select to determine if single or multi)
Single-select: {"type": "SelectOption", "value": ["uuid"]} (length 0 or 1)
Multi-select: {"type": "SelectOption", "value": ["uuid1", "uuid2", ...]} (length 0+)

oneOf.description:
Entity reference(s) - always an array (check is_multi_select to determine if single or multi)
Single-select: {"type": "EntityReference", "value": [{...}]} (length 0 or 1)
Multi-select: {"type": "EntityReference", "value": [{...}, {...}, ...]} (length 0+)

value.description:
Entity reference(s) - always an array (check is_multi_select to determine if single or multi)
Single-select: {"type": "EntityReference", "value": [{...}]} (length 0 or 1)
Multi-select: {"type": "EntityReference", "value": [{...}, {...}, ...]} (length 0+)

oneOf.description:
Link value(s) - always an array (check is_multi_select to determine if single or multi)
Single-select: {"type": "Link", "value": ["https://example.com"]} (length 0 or 1)
Multi-select: {"type": "Link", "value": ["https://example.com", "https://other.com"]} (length 0+)

value.description:
Link value(s) - always an array (check is_multi_select to determine if single or multi)
Single-select: {"type": "Link", "value": ["https://example.com"]} (length 0 or 1)
Multi-select: {"type": "Link", "value": ["https://example.com", "https://other.com"]} (length 0+)

PropertyValue.description:
Property value (service representation).

Represents the actual value stored for an entity property.
This is serialized to/from JSONB in the database.

items.description: The items returned from the call

pinnedItemId.description: The id of the pinned item

path.description: The file path of the bom part content

sha.description:
The sha of the bom part content
There is an index on sha for more performant queries based on it.

modificationData.description:
The modification data for the document instance.
Used to store highlights and other overlays onto a static file

newBom.description:
**DOCX ONLY**
The updated BOM for the document.
Containing the file path and the sha.

sha.description:
The sha of the new document.
This is used to generate a presigned url to upload the new document content to s3.

presignedUrl.description: If the document is an editable file, we provide a presigned url to save the updated file to.

hidden.description:
New value for `crm_companies.hidden`. Setting to `true` hides
the company from CRM listings AND disables `email_sync` (which
permanently deletes the company's contacts and contact sources).
Setting to `false` un-hides the company; `email_sync` is left
untouched and the team must re-enable it explicitly.

New value for `crm_contacts.hidden`. `true` hides the contact
from CRM listings for the team; `false` un-hides it. Display-only
— does not affect populate/depopulate.

email_sync.description:
New value for `crm_companies.email_sync`. Setting to `false`
permanently deletes the company's CRM contacts and contact sources.

oneOf.description: Boolean true/false value
Date and time value
Numeric value
String/text value
Select option by ID (for select-type properties)
Multiple select options by ID (for multi-select properties)
Entity reference
Multiple entity references (for multi-select entity properties)
Link value
Multiple link values (for multi-select link properties)

SetPropertyValue.description: Type-safe enum for setting entity property values - provides compile-time validation.

channelSharePermissions.description: The channel share permissions for the item

isPublic.description: If the item is publicly accessible

oneOf.description: The level of public access

SharedEmailFilter.description: Controls whether shared email threads are included in results.

shortId.description: The short id of the document.

SimpleMention.description: Simple entity mention attached to a message.

SoupCallRecord.description:
A call record as displayed in Soup. Excludes room_name, egress_id,
and transcript — fields that are irrelevant for the soup feed.

attended.description:
Whether the requesting user attended this call (i.e. appears in the
`call_participants` / `call_record_participants` table).

channelId.description: The channel this call belongs to.

channelName.description: Resolved display name for the channel.

customName.description: User-supplied or AI-generated display name for the call.

durationMs.description: Call duration in milliseconds (None if still active).

endedAt.description: When the call ended (None if still active).

isActive.description: Whether the call is currently active.

summary.description:
AI-generated summary of the call. Only set on archived
`call_records` once summarization has run; active calls always
return `None`.

SoupCallRecordParticipant.description: A participant in a call record, as displayed in Soup.

joinedAt.description: When the user joined the call.

leftAt.description: When the user left (None if still in an active call).

createdAt.description: The time the chat was created

deletedAt.description: The time the chat was deleted

isPersistent.description: Whether the chat is persistent or not

projectId.description: The project id of the chat

updatedAt.description: The time the chat was last updated

viewedAt.description: The time the chat was last viewed

SoupCrmCompany.description:
A CRM company as displayed in Soup. Carries the core company
fields plus display metadata resolved from `crm_domain_directory`
against the primary (earliest-created) domain.

description.description: Display description from the primary domain's directory entry.

domains.description:
Domains associated with this company, ordered by creation time
ascending (primary first).

emailSync.description: Whether email sync is enabled for this company.

hidden.description:
Whether the company is hidden from CRM listings. Soup filters
these out by default.

name.description:
Display name from the primary domain's directory entry, or
`None` when unresolved.

teamId.description: The id of the team that owns this company record.

updatedAt.description: When the company was last updated.

SoupCrmDomain.description:
A CRM domain as displayed in Soup. Mirrors the crm crate's
[`CrmDomain`] with a stable wire shape that the FE can rely on.

companyId.description: The id of the company the domain belongs to.

createdAt.description: When the domain record was created.

id.description: The id of the domain record.

branchedFromId.description: The id of the document this document branched from

branchedFromVersionId.description:
The id of the version this document branched from
This could be either DocumentInstance or DocumentBom id depending on the file type

createdAt.description: The time the document was created

deletedAt.description: The time the document was deleted

documentFamilyId.description: The id of the document family this document belongs to

documentVersionId.description:
The version of the document
This could be the document_instance_id or document_bom_id depending on the file type

fileType.description: The file type of the document (e.g. pdf, docx)

projectId.description: The id of the project that this document belongs to

sha.description:
If the document is a PDF, this is the SHA of the pdf
If the document is a DOCX, this will not be present

oneOf.description:
The sub type of the document if present.
Task-related properties are encoded within the variant.

updatedAt.description: The time the document instance / document BOM was updated

viewedAt.description: The time the document was last viewed

oneOf.description: A task document with its associated properties

is_completed.description:
Whether the task is completed.
True if the Status property is set to "Completed".

SoupDocumentSubType.description:
Sub type of a document with associated properties encoded in each variant.
This ensures type-safety: task properties only exist when the document is a task.

SoupForeignEntity.description: A foreign entity record as displayed in Soup.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the record was created.

foreignEntityId.description: Identifier assigned by the external system.

foreignEntitySource.description: Source system that owns the external identifier.

id.description: Internal primary key for this foreign entity record.

metadata.description: Arbitrary metadata stored with the mapping.

storedForAuthEntity.description: Internal auth entity namespace this foreign entity is stored for.

storedForId.description: Internal entity identifier this foreign entity is stored for.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the record was last updated.

createdAt.description: The time the project was created

deletedAt.description: The time the project was deleted

ownerId.description: The user id of who created the project

updatedAt.description: The time the project was updated

viewedAt.description: The time the document was last viewed

SoupProperty.description:
A property attached to a Soup item.

This is a simplified representation that includes only the definition and value,
omitting the entity property assignment metadata and options.

oneOf.description: The property value, if set

TaskFilters.description: Task-only filters nested under document filters.

include_cbm_atm_nc.description:
Include tasks that are created by me, assigned to me, and not completed,
even when they do not match other document filters.

TeamRole.description:
The role a user has within a team.

Ordered least to most privileged so comparisons reflect access strength.

TranscriptSegmentRequest.description: A transcript segment from LiveKit Inference STT.

diarizedSpeakerId.description:
Stable per-speaker identifier produced by the STT provider's diarization
pass. Namespaced upstream by audio track so values are unique across all
tracks in a call. `None` when the provider didn't return a speaker label.

embedding.description:
Speaker voice embedding computed by the transcription agent
(e.g. a Resemblyzer 256-dim vector). When present, the server
upserts a `voice` row and stores the resulting id on the transcript
segment so the call-finished pipeline can match it to enrolled users.

endedAt.description: When the speaker stopped talking for this segment.

isFinal.description: Whether this is a final transcription (not interim).

segmentId.description: LiveKit segment ID (used for deduplication across multiple submitters).

startedAt.description: When the speaker started talking for this segment.

streamStartedAt.description:
Wall-clock when the transcriber's STT stream first received audio
for this participant. The server takes the earliest non-null value
across all participants and uses it to overwrite the
`egress_started`-derived `recording_started_at`, which is too early
(it stamps egress bootstrap, not first audio frame).

items.description: The items returned from the call

oneOf.description:
The access level for the channel
This is required if the operation is add or replace

operation.description:
The type of operation to be performed on the chanel share permission
You can add, remove or replace and existing permission

channelSharePermissions.description: Any channel share permissions to be created/updated/removed

isPublic.description: If the item is publicly accessible

oneOf.description: The level of public access

UploadDocumentStatus.description: The upload status of the document

parentId.description: Optional parent project id to upload the folder into

rootFolderName.description:
The name of the folder you are uploading.

This is used to help us generate the folder map more easily.

next_offset.description: The next offset to be used if there is one

recent.description: The activities returned from the query

total.description: The total number of activities the user has

UserDocumentViewLocation.description: The last place a user viewed a particular document at

next_offset.description: The next offset to be used if there is one

total.description: The total number of documents the user has

recent.description: The pins returned from the query

count.description: Total number of views for the document

users.description: List of user emails who have viewed the document

config.description:
It is an explicit choice that the structure of the view configuration
is up to the frontend. The structure and composition of view configuration
is still very much in flux.

WithCallId.description:
Wrapper carrying just a call id. Used by the [`CallRecordPreview::DoesNotExist`]
variant.

WithChannelId.description: Preview payload returned for channels with only id information.
